channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Mitchell hits tiebreaking HR in the 8th, Chourio goes deep twice and Brewers rally past Mets 5-3
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — Jackson Chourio tied the game in the eighth with his second homer of the night and Garrett Mitchell delivered a two-run shot later in the inning to give the Milwaukee Brewers a 5-3 victory over the New York Mets on Wednesday that evened their NL Wild Card Series.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brewers' 20-year-old rookie Jackson Chourio stepping up his game in postseason
item.description: That $82 million contract Jackson Chourio signed before ever playing a major league game looks like more and more of a bargain with every swing the Milwaukee Brewers rookie takes this postseason.
item.dc:creator: Steve Megargee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Missouri coach Eli Drinkwitz revokes football staffer's access due to brother playing for upcoming opponent
item.description: As Missouri continues to prepare for an SEC showdown against Texas A&M, one member of the Tigers football staff has been locked out of practice sessions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Higashioka's homer starts rally as Padres beat Braves 5-4 to sweep NL Wild Card Series
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Kyle Higashioka's solo homer started a five-run rally against Max Fried with two outs in the second inning, and the San Diego Padres held on to sweep the Atlanta Braves with a 5-4 win in Game 2 of their NL Wild Card Series on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: BERNIE WILSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Orioles get swept out of the playoffs in their only series for a second consecutive year
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Things went so poorly for the Baltimore Orioles in the playoffs that even when they had the bases loaded and a hit by pitch scores a run, Colton Cowser swung at a ball thrown right at him, missed and broke his left hand in the process.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lionel Messi, Inter Miami win MLS Supporters Shield with 3-2 win at Crew. It's Messi's 46th trophy.
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Lionel Messi and Inter Miami have another trophy, along with home-field advantage throughout the Major League Soccer playoffs.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hall of Famer Susie Maxwell Bernie, a 4-time major champion while raising 2 daughters, dies at 83
item.description: PALM SPRINGS, Calif. (AP) — Susie Maxwell Berning, the three-time U.S. Women's Open winner who was a pioneer as a mother while competing on the LPGA Tour, died Wednesday after a two-year battle with lung cancer. She was 83.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Bills place emphasis on defensive backups in wake of NFL's 4-game suspension of Von Miller
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — Bills cornerback Rasul Douglas didn’t think anything of Von Miller's absence at practice on Wednesday, assuming the edge rusher had a rest day, as sometimes happens for the 35-year-old veteran.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Californians think this is the unofficial boundary between Northern and Southern California
item.description: There is no true defining line that represents the distinction between Northern and Southern California, but it’s still a widely popular debate. Some Californians believe the transition from SoCal to NorCal happens when sports fans cheer for the Giants and 49ers as opposed to the Dodgers and Rams.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: College football revamp unveiled with eyes toward the sport's future
item.description: College Sports Tomorrow unveiled its plan for a shake up of the college football landscape with a proposal to move college football out from under the thumb of the NCAA.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dikembe Mutombo, a Hall of Fame player, dies at 58 from brain cancer
item.description: Mutombo, a Basketball Hall of Famer who was one of the best defensive players in NBA history, died Monday.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The final day for the Oakland Athletics arrives ahead of next season's move away from the Bay
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — When manager Mark Kotsay removed his Oakland jersey for the final time Sunday, it was bound for the Baseball Hall of Fame. Brent Rooker? His final jersey with the name Oakland across the front will be preserved in a frame and displayed on his wall at home.
item.dc:creator: TIM BOOTH, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Utes offense struggles in 23-10 loss to Arizona
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The absence of Cam Rising finally caught up to the Utah football team, as the Utes offense produced just one touchdown and one field goal in a 23-10 loss to Arizona Saturday night at Rice-Eccles Stadium.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Taillon pitches 7 crisp innings as the Cubs blank the Reds 1-0
item.description: Jameson Taillon pitched seven crisp innings in his second straight win, and the Chicago Cubs beat the Cincinnati Reds 1-0 on Friday in the opener of their season-ending series.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Former BYU track and field sprinter dies in mountain biking crash
item.description: A former Brigham Young University track and field sprinter died Wednesday afternoon after a tragic mountain biking accident, according to a university press release.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, ABC 4

item.title: Los Angeles Chargers to sell minority stake in team to Detroit Pistons owner
item.description: More than a quarter stake in the Los Angeles Chargers, which has essentially been a family business for decades, will be sold to an NBA owner in the coming weeks, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: What's next for the Oakland Coliseum now that the A's are gone?
item.description: (KRON) -- After 57 years at the Oakland Coliseum, the Oakland Athletics played their final game there on Thursday, giving the fans something to celebrate with a 3-2 win over the Texas Rangers in front of a sell-out crowd.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Steve Stone signs long-term deal to remain a White Sox broadcaster
item.description: Steve Stone has signed a long-term contract to remain in the booth as a broadcaster for the Chicago White Sox, marking his 18th season with the team.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Meet the champions: These are the top stars of WWE NXT on the CW Network
item.description: Who is the future of sports entertainment? To answer that question, one must look to the top tier of NXT, the champions who have overcome the odds and claimed bragging rights over every superstar on the brand.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, ABC 4

item.title: Derrick Rose, Chicago native and NBA MVP with the Bulls, announces retirement from pro basketball after 16 seasons
item.description: CHICAGO — Derrick Rose, a Chicago native and city basketball legend in every sense of the word, is retiring from professional basketball. Rose, who grew up in Englewood on the city's Southwest Side and was a prep star at Simeon before being drafted No.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Oakland A's play last game at Coliseum Thursday afternoon
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (KRON) -- After 57 years of Major League Baseball, the Oakland A's will play their last game at the Oakland Coliseum on Thursday afternoon. They will take on the Texas Rangers at 12:37 p.m. The A's are headed to Sacramento next season, and they plan to eventually go to Las Vegas.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4
